summary: Speed optimization and mobile/tablet viewport testing complete. Production build dramatically improved page load times (Calculator: 16.6s→196ms, Services: 2.0s→252ms). Mobile responsive design verified: MobileTabBar, AURA button, WhatsApp button properly positioned. Two pages have horizontal overflow issues on mobile.

minor.issue: 429 rate limiting errors on multiple requests - CDN/server limitation, not code issue

ui_bugs.issue: Horizontal overflow 34-152px on mobile (390px viewport). H1 element 'PROJECT CALCULATOR' causing overflow
Horizontal overflow 49px on mobile (390px viewport). Large text in hero section causing overflow

mobile_viewport_390px.mobile_tab_bar_tabs: PASS - Home, Builder, My Projects, Services, Contact
mobile_viewport_390px.aura_above_whatsapp: PASS - AURA button stacked above WhatsApp

widget_functionality.whatsapp_panel_responsive: PASS - Panel width is responsive (not fixed 420px)
widget_functionality.aura_chat_sends_message: PASS - Messages send and receive responses

action_items: Fix Calculator page horizontal overflow on mobile - add responsive text sizing to H1 'PROJECT CALCULATOR' (text-5xl → text-3xl sm:text-5xl or add overflow-hidden/break-words)
Fix Products page horizontal overflow on mobile - reduce hero H1 text size (text-7xl sm:text-8xl → text-4xl sm:text-7xl md:text-8xl)
critical_code_review_comments: Calculator page H1 (line 522-523): 'text-5xl sm:text-6xl' without mobile breakpoint causes overflow
Products page H1 (line 53): 'text-7xl sm:text-8xl' too large for 390px viewport without word-wrap
Production build successfully implemented with 'next build && next start -p 3000'
Mobile responsive positioning for floating buttons correctly uses Tailwind responsive classes (bottom-[168px] lg:bottom-[104px])
context_for_next_testing_agent: Speed optimization confirmed working. Two overflow issues remain: Calculator and Products pages have horizontal overflow on mobile due to large H1 text. Fix requires adding responsive text classes or overflow handling. Rate limiting (429) errors are server-side, not code issues.
